PR surface: Source +17, Tests +42. Total +59 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: Partly: the unbounded prompt concatenation is source-reproducible from current main, but I did not re-run the live model attribution symptom. The linked issue provides release-level symptom detail and this PR targets the implicated join/composition paths.

target.title: fix(agents): bound plugin system context
target.body:
Fixes #87045.

## Summary
- Adds a small wrapper for plugin-injected system context.
- Labels the block as plugin-provided context instead of workspace file content.
- Applies the wrapper to CLI and embedded prompt-building hook paths while leaving OpenClaw-owned active task prompts unwrapped.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Plugin hook system context is now bounded and attributed so model-visible prompt text does not look like raw workspace file content.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux at PR head `a53cf20890fac506870e8bb7c6c946ac6259a798`, with workspace dependencies installed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
pnpm exec tsx -e "import { wrapPluginSystemContextSection } from './src/agents/hook-system-context-boundary.ts'; const out = wrapPluginSystemContextSection('  ## Rules  \r\nFoo\t\r\n'); if (!out?.includes('not workspace file content') || !out.includes('## Rules\nFoo') || !out.startsWith('---') || !out.endsWith('---')) throw new Error(String(out)); console.log(out);"
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The actual wrapper code normalizes the plugin text, surrounds it with an explicit boundary, and includes attribution that it is plugin-injected system context rather than workspace file content.
- **What was not tested**: A live model response attribution check; the proof above exercises the actual prompt transformation code in the OpenClaw checkout.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the patched OpenClaw checkout:
> ---
>
> OpenClaw plugin-injected system context. This block is not workspace file content.
>
> ## Rules
> Foo
>
> ---

## Supplemental checks
- `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
